titleInfo.title: Cliff Sculptures of Bishui Monastery in Mianyang

mods.abstract: 碧水寺在綿陽市區涪江北岸的碧水崖邊，背依龟(龜?)山 Jade Water Temple is situated in the city of Mianyang on the northern bank of Fu River, Mount Gui in its back. The temple was built right in front of Mount Gui’s cliff; it has altogether 18 niches, 2 on the south wall, 16 on the west wall. The article gives a description of the sculptures of in niches 1, 4, 5, and 16, one by one, and of the Diamond sutra. Niche 1 contains Buddha Amitabha, Guanyin and Dashizhi.Niche 4 same identification. Niche 5 contains altogether 44 figures; three of those are the main trias. Some of the remaining 41 figures have been substituted in clay. Originally, it should have been more than 41 subsidiary figures, but how many have been setroyed is hard to say. Main Buddha in niche 16 is also identified as Amitabha. Diamond sutra has more than 4000 characters craved in grid, in kaishu. One fifth of the characters are already weathered. 《綿陽縣誌。奇現》as text source. The sculptures are said to date in the Tang dynasty because its style is close to the sculpture in caves Wanfodong 万佛洞, Laolongdong 老龍洞 and 惠簡洞 Huijiandong in Longmen. The sculptures in niche 1 are likened to a Guanyin dated 681 (Gaozong Yonglong 2)outside of Wanfodong 万佛洞. Besides, the sculptures of Bishuisi are similar to the Daoist figures of Yunüquan 玉女泉. Many of those carry votiv inscriptions; therefore it is known that they were made between 610 and 871. The 《綿陽縣誌。奇現》has the following report: 大观元年(1107年)冬与表弟都奉郎李益侯。。。。观先辈郡守李同叔所建水阁院(即现碧水寺)。 北崖， 正观时令狐文轨施造经像，极工致，叹其历会昌以来渐暗淡，遂醵金彩绘，焕然生色。, testifying that in 1107 the niches of Bishuisi were restaurated.Gilding traces in niche 5 are linked to this Song restoration. The 《綿陽縣誌。金刚》further has “字体秀逸，是隋唐间笔意“. Note that the 《綿陽縣誌》either makes a mistake in transcribing the name of the alleged donor,狐文轨, or the Song donors made a mistake in transcribing this name’s second character. A more recent report (Yu_Wang2009)could identify the donor as 狐元軌 whose activities have been transmitted in several miracle stories among the Buddhist canon. The present report also gives a description of the stone cut Guanyin that is 3,13 m high. It was excavated in Kaiyuansi 開元寺 and moved to Bishuisi. The 《綿陽縣誌》also has a report on the sculptures of Kaiyuansi saying that „有正观，总章，会昌等年号“. Therefore, the Bishuisi Guanyin is held to be a famous image of the Tang dynasty. Bishuisi was called 水阁院 during the Tang and Song dynasties. In 1800 (´Qing Jiaqing 5) a hall was built on top of the ruins, and during the renovation of 1806 (Jiaqing 11) it received its present name.
